description: Core guidelines that apply to ALL AI-Hydro tasks and queries

rules.title: ALWAYS Use Clean, Transformed Field Names
rules.explanation: AI-Hydro tools transform raw API field names into clean, standardized names. You MUST use the clean names returned by AI-Hydro functions.

correct: result['gauge_id']  # Clean name for USGS site identifier
result['gauge_name']  # Clean name for station name
result['area_km2']  # Clean name for area in km²
incorrect: result['site_no']  # WRONG - This is raw NWIS field
result['station_nm']  # WRONG - This is raw NWIS field
result['dec_lat_va']  # WRONG - This is raw NWIS field

rules.rationale: Raw API field names are inconsistent and unclear. Clean names follow CAMELS conventions and are self-documenting.
rules.enforcement: Check tool documentation for exact return field names before accessing

rules.title: ALWAYS Check Tool Documentation for Return Fields
rules.explanation: Before accessing any field in a return value, consult the tool's 'returns.fields' documentation to confirm the exact field name.
workflow: 1. Read tool documentation to see 'returns.fields' section
2. Note the exact field names (e.g., 'gauge_id' not 'site_no')
3. Use ONLY the documented field names in your code
4. Never guess or assume field names based on external APIs
rules.common_mistake: Assuming tool returns match raw API field names (they don't - they're transformed)

rules.title: Use 'gdf' for GeoDataFrames, NOT 'watershed'
rules.explanation: Watershed geometry is returned as 'gdf' (GeoDataFrame), not 'watershed'
rules.rationale: 'gdf' is explicit about the data type (GeoDataFrame)

rules.title: Verify Fields Exist Before Accessing
rules.explanation: Not all tools return all fields. Check documentation for what's actually available.

rules.title: Always use MCP tools — never import Python directly
rules.explanation: AI-Hydro tools are exposed through the ai-hydro MCP server. Always call them as MCP tools.
rules.correct_pattern: delineate_watershed(gauge_id='01031500', workspace_dir='/path')  # call MCP tool directly
incorrect_patterns: from ai_hydro.tools.watershed import delineate_watershed  # WRONG - use MCP tool
from hydrocatch import delineate_watershed  # WRONG - hydrocatch is deprecated
rules.execution: Call MCP tools directly — never write Python scripts for ai_hydro analysis

rules.title: Follow CAMELS Naming Conventions
rules.explanation: AI-Hydro follows CAMELS attribute naming conventions for consistency
rules.note: These names are self-documenting and widely used in hydrology literature

before_accessing_fields: 3. Use exact field names from docs

KeyError.cause: Using raw API field name instead of clean name
KeyError.solution: Check tool documentation for correct field name (likely 'gauge_id')

unexpected_return.solution: Read tool's 'returns.fields' to see what's actually available
